channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: NASA selects Eric Schmidt’s rocket company for a 2028 mission to Mars
item.description: Relativity Space, the rocket company led by former Google executive Eric Schmidt, was picked to launch NASA's Aeolus payload to Mars in 2028, as reported earlier by TechCrunch. Under a new public-private partnership, Relativity Space will provide the "spacecraft, rocket, and cruise operations" to fly Aeolus to Mars, where the payload will "provide the first […]...

item.title: Do fitness trackers still work if you have tattoos?
item.description: The short answer is: sometimes, but it's complicated.

item.title: 'We've proven that carbon removal technology can work': Google, Anthropic, Salesforce and others pledge nearly $1 billion on new environmental push
item.description: Google, Anthropic and others commit to buying future carbon removal credits to help today's startups get funding.

item.title: LifeLock's 'intuitive and powerful' identity theft protection is highly rated for a reason — LifeLock Advanced can secure your entire family, and it's on sale
item.description: Up to $1 million in ID theft renumeration, $100,000 for stolen funds, financial account activity alerts, data removal, and 24/7 customer phone support

item.title: Dutton Ranch season 2 'deserves to be made' according to one star — confirming Yellowstone spinoff cast is 'waiting in hope' for Paramount+ renewal
item.description: With two episodes of the first installment still to go, there's no word on Dutton Ranch season 2 — but one star wants 'at least' four seasons.

item.title: Apple MacBook Neo, iPhone 17 Pro Max et traceur Ugreen FineTrack : les meilleures promos high-tech à saisir ce vendredi — meilleurs bons plans
item.description: Vendredi 19 juin 2026 marque une journée riche en promotions sur des produits très recherchés : voici celles à retenir.

item.title: Americans celebrate Juneteenth as Obama’s presidential center opens in Chicago
item.description: This is the fifth year since Juneteenth was designated as a federal holiday. As people gather across the U.S. to celebrate Juneteenth on Friday, former President Barack Obama’s presidential center is opening its doors to the general public for the first time.

item.title: 16GB 'Bonkers' Xeon PC is back at Newegg for Prime Day with $209 price tag, Nvidia Geforce GPU, 256GB SSD - Is this the best PC deal of 2026?
item.description: Newegg's weird mini PC divided opinions, but is now back with a vengeance

item.title: Hue’s wired wall modules bring non-smart lights into its ecosystem
item.description: Smart lighting company Philips Hue has launched its first wired wall modules. Installed behind existing wall switches, the new devices bring non-smart lights into the Hue ecosystem for the first time. Hue also announced new Play table and floor lamps that are more affordable versions of its Signe series, along with upgrades to its E14 […]

item.title: Infowars is returning with Tim Heidecker at the helm. Heres what it’ll look like.
item.description: The Onion will relaunch Infowars on July 2 with comedian Tim Heidecker as creative director.

item.title: Norway imposes broad restrictions on AI for elementary school kids
item.description: Norway will reportedly ban young kids from using AI in schools.

item.title: ‘Workers need to be involved in these conversations’: Amazon engineers to be investigated after criticising company’s AI data center buildout – Amazon “may or may not take action based on what we find.”
item.description: A group of Amazon employees spoke out at a council meeting, and now some of them are being investigated.

item.title: J’ai testé la Sony Bravia Theatre Bar 7 : la barre Atmos compacte qui réclame son caisson
item.description: Avec la Bravia Theatre Bar 7, Sony propose une barre de son Dolby Atmos compacte pensée comme une solution home-cinéma simple, mais évolutive avec caisson et enceintes arrière optionnels. Je l’ai essayée seule et avec le caisson Bravia Theatre 7 Sub.

item.title: America just buried a message for people living in 2276. Some of the items inside are wild
item.description: The 900-pound capsule contains more than 200 items, from AI predictions and gambling chips to whale bones and Olympic memorabilia. A collection of treasures including a molecular safety deposit box and a glass Coke bottle were encased in metal this week, not to be opened for another 250 years.
